title: cacalia bahamensis
intro: Cacalia bahamensis, also known as Bahamian cacalia or Bahamian button, is a perennial plant native to the Bahamas and southern Florida. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, primarily as a diuretic to treat kidney stones and to relieve symptoms of urinary tract infections. The plant's roots and leaves contain flavonoids, which may contribute to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, providing potential health benefits such as reduced inflammation and improved cardiovascular health. Cacalia bahamensis can be prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, but its use should be consulted with a healthcare professional due to potential interactions with other medications or underlying health conditions.
